data.title: ZEEKR Intelligent is on watch as early investors push back on Geely bid
data.description:
Five early investors in ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited (NYSE:ZK) have expressed to the board in at least two letters that Geely is undervaluing the electric vehicle company with its $2.2 billion take-private offer, according to Reuters.

Sources indicated that the investors voicing their stance on the offer are Contemporary Amperex Technology Co Ltd, Intel Capital (Intel's (INTC) venture capital arm), Boyu Capital, Bilibili (BILI), and Cathay Fortune Corp.

Geely made the offer of $25.66 per ZEEKR ADS (NYSE:ZK) on May 7. The price represented a premium of 13.6% over ZEEKR's (ZK) closing price before the announcement and a 24% premium to its four-week average share price before the offer. Geely already owns 65.7% of ZEEKR (ZK). Geely plans to finance the acquisition using a mix of cash, new shares, and potentially debt.

If the deal closes, Geely aims to fully merge ZEEKR (ZK) into Geely Auto to streamline its equity structure, consolidate resources, reduce duplication, and enhance competitiveness in the global EV market.

Shares of ZEEKR (ZK) were up 1.5% in premarket trading on Friday to $27.00. ZEEKR's (ZK) IPO was priced at $21 per ADS on May 10, 2024.

data.title: Stingray CFO takes medical leave of absence, Marie-Hélène Fournier appointed interim CFO
data.description:
Stingray Group (OTCPK:STGYF) (RAY.A:CA) (RAY.B:CA) said on Friday that CFO Jean-Pierre Trahan is taking a leave of absence for health reasons.

Marie-Hélène Fournier is appointed interim CFO, effective immediately.

The company said Fournier has been a mainstay over the past 15 years, has led or taken part in over 75 acquisitions and direct investment transactions.

data.title: SmartStop Self Storage REIT to be added to Russell 3000 index
Modine to acquire L.B. White
data.description:
• Modine (NYSE:MOD) announced on Friday that it had entered into a definitive agreement to acquire L.B. White.
  • Modine expects the transaction to close May 31, 2025.
  • L.B. White expects to report revenue of $73.5 million for their fiscal 2025-year ending May 31. Total consideration for the transaction is based on an enterprise value of approximately $112 million.
  • Management expects this acquisition to be immediately accretive to earnings and anticipates generating incremental growth and future cost savings through capturing synergies and deploying Modine's 80/20 operating model.
  • Upon close, L.B. White will be integrated into Modine's HVAC Technologies product group and the current leadership team will remain in place, said the company. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eos Energy slides further after pricing $225 million convertible note offering
data.description:
Eos Energy Enterprises (NASDAQ:EOSE) extended its losses after announcing the pricing of a $225 million offering of 6.75% convertible senior notes due 2030 on Friday.

Eos Energy Enterprises (NASDAQ:EOSE) shares fell 7% premarket after plunging 25% in the previous session.

The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $175 million aggregate principal amount of notes.

Separately, Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) announced the pricing of an offering of 18,750,000 shares of common stock at a public offering price of $4.00 per share.

The net proceeds from the offering are expected to be $70.5 million—or $81.1 million if the underwriters fully exercise their option to purchase additional shares—after deducting underwriting discounts and commissions.

data.title: Dell's results and guidance highlight strong execution, continued AI momentum: analysts
data.description:
Strong results and even better guidance from Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) are indicative that the Round Rock, TX.-based company is navigating tariff-related concerns well and gaining more than its fair share of artificial intelligence spending, Wall Street analysts said.

Shares rose 0.5% in premarket trading.

“Despite tariff-related headwinds, DELL executed well, limiting tariff costs and maintaining pricing (while peers raised prices), highlighting materially better execution vs. peers,” Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani, who has an Outperform rating and $140 price target on Dell, wrote in a note to clients. Daryanani also highlighted momentum in its AI server business, as the company ended the quarter with a $5B sequential boost to its backlog to $14.4B. “We think DELL is seeing share gains here despite being the premium provider as customers are willing to pay a premium price for better execution [and] supply chain strength,” he posited.

“We come away impressed following DELL’s execution amid a noisy backdrop,” Daryanani added. “While AI server shipments are expected to normalize in H2, we think there will be upside to estimates if DELL were to further grow its backlog.” 

Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers reiterated his Overweight rating and $150 price target, as he sees the results and guidance supportive of his thesis that Dell is “well positioned” for the Blackwell cycle from Nvidia (NVDA).

The company is also executing well in its supply chain; it's benefiting from the commercial PC upgrade cycle; and it's generating free cash flow and returning cash to shareholders, Rakers said.

Morgan Stanley analyst Erik Woodring, who has an Overweight rating on Dell, was a bit more cautious in his praise. However, he upped his price target to $135 from $126 after the results and guidance, citing continued AI server momentum. 

“Without high-margin AI server attach, DELL's multiple likely peaks at 12-13x, but a path to positive EPS revisions means outperformance can persist,” Woodring wrote in a note to clients.

data.title: Barrick seeks help from World Bank court on government takeover of Mali mine
data.description:
Barrick Mining (NYSE:B) has asked the World Bank's arbitration tribunal to intervene in legal proceedings in Mali, Reuters reported late Thursday, as the company faces the possibility of the Loulo-Gounkoto gold mine falling under the control of the Mali government.

Barrick's (NYSE:B) request for "provisional measures" from the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes comes as a local court in Mali is expected to rule on June 2 on the government's request to put the company's mine under a provisional administration; if the Mali court ruling goes in favor of the government, an individual or an organization would be assigned to take over the mine and reopen it.

An official familiar with the Mali government's thinking told Reuters that there are no grounds for international arbitration, and it is a domestic tax issue; even if the World Bank tribunal rules in favor of Barrick's (B) request for a provisional measure, the government could ignore it, although Mali would risk further scaring off Western investors.

Barrick's (B) Loulo-Gounkoto mine has been shut since January after Mali seized three tons of gold over non-payment of taxes, an allegation the company has denied.

data.title: CollPlant Biotechnologies shares slide after announces capital raise $3.6M through registered direct offering
Digital identity: EU reveals upcoming age-verification app
data.description:
The "EU Digital Identity Wallet" is set to roll out next year, which will offer the ability for citizens to prove who they are online without giving over their information to external websites. The effort is being aimed at an increasingly digitized world, and will impact everything from travel to ordering prescriptions from pharmacies. The wallet would also give the ability to store verified electronic documents, like tax forms, notarized filings, and personal work certifications, as well as driving licenses and payment cards.

Backdrop: The latest project followed the adoption of the eIDAS regulation in 2014. In 2021, the European Commission proposed a revision called the European Digital Identity Framework, which was updated last year to lay down the rules for the core functionalities and certification of eID Wallets. The latter will be recognized throughout Europe, assisting with domestic and cross-border transactions without the need to remember countless passwords. 

Ahead of the launch of the "EU Digital Identity Wallet," the bloc just revealed that a new white label age-verification app will be out by the summer. The latter could allow for stricter policing and protections for minors online, helping to shield them from accessing harmful content and websites. Services like Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Meta (NASDAQ:META) are already being probed by the EU under the Digital Markets Act, and the latest push toward digital identification may give fresh impetus to those investigations.

Privacy concerns? It may only be a matter of time before the "EU Digital Identity Wallet" is required by the government or is demanded by the private sector as a strong form of identification. Untold amounts of personal information, transactions, and other data - all stored under one framework - could become a hacker's paradise despite selective disclosure capabilities. There are also worries about mass surveillance and the potential for rating systems or scores, though others say that ship has sailed a long time ago for any modern digital economy.

data.title: Ralph Lauren attracts a bull rating from Deutsche Bank
data.description:
Deutsche Bank resumed coverage on Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) with a Buy rating in a note titled "Not Your Grandpa's Polo." 

Analyst Krisztina Katai highlighted that few companies within the DB's coverage fit the description of high-quality with good underlying fundamentals, pricing power, white space opportunity, and limited China sourcing, but the firm thinks Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) checks all the boxes.

"We believe the market still underappreciates the transformational changes, distribution and sourcing model, and margin expansion potential, but a positive EPS revision cycle ahead should help move both estimates and valuation higher."

Ralph Lauren's (RL) Q1 results and FQ1 trends were noted to further increase DB's confidence. The luxury apparel maker's sound balance sheet, attractive free cash flow yield, and robust buyback program are seen helping to support double-digit earnings growth.

Deutsche Bank assigned a price target of $343 to Ralph Lauren (RL), implying 25% potential upside including the dividend yield. 

Shares of Ralph Lauren (RL) were up 0.3% in premarket trading to $278.37 vs. the 52-week range of $155.96 to $289.33. Short interest on RL stands at 7.1% of the total float.
